pr.title: Gitignore .dump files from the asmdiff script
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Added a repository-level .gitignore rule for *.dump files produced by the asmdiff workflow, preventing temporary comparison artifacts from appearing as untracked files during decompilation matching work.

key_files.reason: Added the *.dump ignore pattern under the Temporary files section.

what_changed: Placed the new ignore rule in the existing `# Temporary files` section alongside `*.swp`.
The PR title identifies these `.dump` files as outputs from the asmdiff script.
smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal: one targeted ignore pattern instead of changing asmdiff behavior.
Grouped the rule with other temporary-file ignores, making the repository hygiene convention easy to find.
Reduced noise for contributors running assembly diff tooling during matching work.
decomp_lessons: Matching tools often generate disposable artifacts; ignore those artifacts at the repository level when they are common to the workflow.
Small repository-hygiene fixes can improve decomp velocity by keeping `git status` focused on intentional source changes.
When adding ignores for tooling outputs, place them in an existing semantic section such as temporary files rather than scattering patterns.

naming_conventions.meaning: Temporary dump files generated by the asmdiff script, according to the PR title.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use asmdiff-generated `.dump` files as temporary local artifacts only; they should not be committed.
Keep generated comparison output out of version control so matching diffs remain about source and assembly changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify asmdiff output handling or ignore additional generated matching artifacts.
Search for other generated temporary file extensions that may need repository-level ignore rules.
Check whether documentation for asmdiff mentions `.dump` outputs and local cleanup expectations.
